concerns.text: Research question asks 'Do heterogeneous models achieve better performance' (causal claim), but plan uses correlational design (paired t-test across tasks) without randomization or confound control. Research.md acknowledges 'Causal inference: Observational comparison; claims framed as associational' — this mismatch between causal question and correlational design undermines validity.

Sample size of 20 tasks with 5 seeds (100 total observations) lacks power analysis. No effect size estimate or variance assumption provided to justify detectability of the claimed performance difference. SC-001 defers threshold to '[deferred] %' — without pilot data or power calculation, cannot verify sample adequacy.

Unified condition converts time-series/tabular to text before LLM inference. This translation step introduces information loss confound — poorer performance may reflect translation artifacts rather than architectural limitation. No control condition isolates translation effect from architecture effect.

Task selection bias unaddressed. 20 tasks drawn from heterogeneous datasets (UCI, MUST, DROP, US-100K) with varying difficulty, modality balance, and label quality. No stratification or blocking to control for task-level confounders that could drive observed differences.
